title: See the Odd-Looking Barreleye Fish in Its Natural Habitat for the First Time
gist: A 35-day Schmidt Ocean Institute expedition to the Doldrums Fracture Zone off Brazil discovered two new hydrothermal vent fields hosting chemosynthetic ecosystems and captured the barreleye fish in its natural habitat for the first time.

insights.claim: A 35-day Schmidt Ocean Institute expedition to the Doldrums Fracture Zone, 800 miles off northeast Brazil, discovered two new hydrothermal vent fields teeming with life.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Adds to the map of active deep-sea vent ecosystems.
insights.locator: Scientists from the Schmidt Ocean Institute

insights.claim: The vent ecosystem is built on chemosynthetic bacteria that draw energy from vent chemicals rather than sunlight, supporting ghostly anemones, crabs, and blind shrimp.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Reinforces chemosynthesis as the base of sunless deep-sea food webs.

insights.claim: The expedition captured the odd-looking barreleye fish in its natural habitat for the first time.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Notable first observation of a rarely seen species.
